svg.title: MN 26 Pāsarāsi Sutta — The Noble Quest
svg.desc: A discourse visualization of MN 26: two kinds of quest, the noble search begins, two teachers and the doctrinal verdict, full awakening at Uruvelā as attainment and as knowledge, Brahmā's appeal, and the meditative path beyond Māra's reach.

g.text: Seeking the unborn, unsurpassed security from bondage
TWO KINDS OF QUESTS · DVEMĀ PARIYESANĀ
Being subject to what changes, one either seeks more of it — or, having understood the drawback, seeks release
“ children, spouse, servants, livestock, gold and silver — all subject to birth, aging, illness, death ”
One seeks what is subject to birth
One seeks what is subject to aging
One seeks what is subject to illness
One seeks what is subject to death
One seeks what is subject to sorrow
One seeks what is subject to defilement
One seeks the unborn unsurpassed security from bondage
THE NOBLE SEARCH BEGINS · ARIYĀ PARIYESANĀ
Realization, then resolve, then going forth — the going-forth is the result, not the topic
“Why am I, being subject to birth, seeking what is also subject to birth?”
kiṁ nu kho ahaṁ jātidhammo samāno jātidhammaṁyeva pariyesāmi?
“Suppose, having understood the drawback, I seek the unborn, unsurpassed security from bondage”
ādīnavaṁ viditvā, ajātaṁ anuttaraṁ yogakkhemaṁ nibbānaṁ pariyeseyyaṁ
Then, while still young, with black hair, he shaved hair and beard, put on the ochre robes, and went forth
kesamassuṁ ohāretvā kāsāyāni vatthāni acchādetvā agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajiṁ
TWO TEACHERS · ATTAINMENT AND SUBSEQUENT DEPARTURE
The most refined samādhis still leads to re-appearance in subtle realms — not Nibbāna
The Base of Nothingness — the highest his teacher knew
“ This Dhamma does not lead to nibbāna —
only to re-appearance in this base ”
The Base of Neither Perception nor Non-Perception
“ This Dhamma does not lead to nibbāna —
only to re-appearance in this base ”
AT URUVELĀ — THE NOBLE QUEST FULFILLED · SAMBODHI
The same six rows — now resolved. And the knowledge: this is final
“I attained the unborn, unsurpassed security from bondage.”
there is no further renewed existence ”
A real hesitation, then a turning toward the world
“ My mind inclined toward being unconcerned, not toward teaching the Dhamma ”
“ The Dhamma is profound — but there are beings with little dust in their eyes ”
santi sattā apparajakkhajātikā, assavaṇatā dhammassa parihāyanti
“ The doors to the Deathless are open" — apārutā tesaṁ amatassa dvārā
WHERE MĀRA CANNOT GO · AGATI MĀRASSA
“ has blinded Māra, gone beyond the Evil One's sight, becoming invisible to him ”
andhamakāsi māraṁ, apadaṁ vadhitvā māracakkhuṁ adassanaṁ gato pāpimato
Secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome states
Born of seclusion (vivekajaṁ) · Imbued with uplifting joy and pleasure (pītisukhaṁ)
Free from reflection and examination (avitakkaṁ · avicāraṁ)
Born of collectedness (samādhijaṁ) · Imbued with uplifting joy and pleasure (pītisukhaṁ)
With fading of desire for uplifting joy (pītiyā ca virāgā)
Experiencing pleasure with the body (sukhaṁ ca kāyena paṭisaṁvedeti)
Abandoning bodily pleasure and pain (sukhassa ca pahānā · dukkhassa ca pahānā)
Settling of mental pleasure and displeasure (somanassadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamā)
Base of Neither Perception Nor Non-Perception
Cessation of Perception and What Is Felt
Having seen with wisdom, his mental defilements are completely exhausted
paññāya cassa disvā āsavā parikkhīṇā honti
He has crossed over entanglement in the world
